description_long: This is a pair of garden pruning shears with two robust, curved metal blades joined by a central pivot and spring mechanism. The handles are elongated, cylindrical, and coated in a reddish-brown material, likely for improved grip and comfort during use. The blades are dark, metallic, and show signs of wear, suggesting frequent use for cutting branches or stems. The spring between the handles ensures the blades automatically open after each cut, enhancing efficiency. The overall construction appears sturdy, with the metal components providing strength and the coated handles offering ergonomic support. No visible branding or logos are present on the tool.
description: A sturdy pair of garden pruning shears with curved metal blades and reddish-brown coated handles, featuring a central spring mechanism.
description_view_0: The shears are shown with both handles and blades clearly visible, highlighting the curved blades and the spring mechanism.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the open position of the blades and the spring, with the cutting edges more prominent.
description_view_2: The handles are more spread apart, showing the full length of the reddish-brown grips and the curvature of the blades.
description_view_3: The shears are viewed from the back, focusing on the spring and the rear ends of the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical, coated handles with a reddish-brown, likely plastic or rubberized, ergonomic grip.
attribute_jaw_design: Curved, bypass-style blades with a sharp cutting edge, designed for pruning; no serrations or wire cutter features.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central pivot with a visible coil spring for automatic opening; does not allow adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth, matte finish on the handle coating, providing a non-slip, comfortable grip.
